description: Desert Rose (Adenium obesum) is a succulent ornamental plant toxic to cats due to cardenolides — potent cardiac glycosides concentrated throughout the entire plant, including the sap, roots, stems, and flowers. These compounds inhibit the sodium-potassium ATPase pump in heart muscle cells, disrupting the electrical signals that regulate heartbeat rhythm.
name: Desert Rose
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: cardenolides (cardiac glycosides), similar in action to digitalis
Mechanism: Na+/K+-ATPase inhibition causing cardiac conduction disruption and arrhythmias
Symptoms: drooling, vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy, weakness, abnormal heart rate (bradycardia or arrhythmia), collapse
All parts are toxic; the milky sap poses the greatest risk if ingested or contacted
Even small exposures warrant veterinary assessment due to cardiac risk
